perturbation.original_text: All disclosures of "Proprietary Information" will be in writing and marked "PROPRIETARY' or equivalent words by ADC at the time such writings are first furnished to BATTELLE.
perturbation.changed_text: All disclosures of "Proprietary Information" may be oral and need not be marked "PROPRIETARY' or equivalent words by ADC at the time such writings are first furnished to BATTELLE.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "will be in writing and marked" to "may be oral and need not be marked" directly contradicts the requirement for written and marked proprietary information. This in-text contradiction introduces uncertainty about what information is covered under the agreement. If the disclosure doesn't have to be in writing, there's a problem for tracking compliance with the rule set in Article 3, and Article 5.
perturbation.contradicted_text: BATTELLE and its representative(s) shall maintain the identified Proprietary Information in confidence for a period of three (3) years from the effective date of this Agreement. During this period, BATTELLE shall not divulge such information to any third party or use such information for any purpose other than review and evaluation without the prior written consent of ADC. BATTELLE shall treat such information with the same degree of care as it accords to its own proprietary information.
perturbation.justification: The original text required all disclosures of Proprietary Information to be in writing and marked 'PROPRIETARY'. The changed text makes disclosures optional ('may be oral') and removes the marking requirement. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that 'BATTELLE and its representative(s) shall maintain the identified Proprietary Information in confidence.' If information can be disclosed orally and is not required to be marked as proprietary, it creates a significant conflict. This makes it unclear which information BATTELLE is obligated to keep confidential. The changed text directly breaks compatibility with the contradicted text, which relies on 'identified Proprietary Information,' implying a written and marked form. Therefore, this is a strong in-text contradiction according to the structural flaws definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: Proprietary Information disclosed hereunder shall remain the property of ADC.
perturbation.changed_text: Proprietary Information disclosed hereunder shall become the property of BATTELLE.
perturbation.explanation: Changing ownership of proprietary information from ADC to BATTELLE creates a fundamental contradiction. This introduces uncertainty concerning who has rights to the information disclosed. This in-text contradiction directly impacts the 'no license' provision because you cannot grant yourself a license.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Proprietary Information disclosed hereunder shall become the property of BATTELLE. No license under any patent, copyright, trademark or trade secret is granted or implied.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that Proprietary Information becomes the property of BATTELLE, while the contradicted text also states that Proprietary Information becomes the property of BATTELLE, yet no license is granted or implied. Although these two statements appear to be in agreement, the explanation states that by changing the ownership from ADC to BATTELLE, there is a direct impact on the 'no license' provision because BATTELLE cannot grant itself a license. This creates a logical inconsistency and a strong in-text contradiction, hence 'YES'.

perturbation.original_text: BATTELLE shall treat such information with the same degree of care as it accords to its own proprietary information.
perturbation.changed_text: BATTELLE shall not be required to treat such information with any degree of care.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the obligation for BATTELLE to treat the information with care directly contradicts the purpose of the NDA. This introduces uncertainty as it voids a key component of what's suppose to protect the information. This in-text contradiction creates concerns about how it is conflicting with Article 3 of the document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: BATTELLE and its representative(s) shall maintain the identified Proprietary Information in confidence for a period of three (3) years from the effective date of this Agreement. During this period, BATTELLE shall not divulge such information to any third party or use such information for any purpose other than review and evaluation without the prior written consent of ADC. BATTELLE shall not be required to treat such information with any degree of care.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires BATTELLE to treat information with the same degree of care as its own proprietary information. The changed text states that BATTELLE is not required to treat such information with any degree of care. The contradicted text includes the sentence 'BATTELLE shall not be required to treat such information with any degree of care'. The change directly introduces a clause that BATTELLE has no obligation, contradicting the expectation that it must be held in confidence, as initially stated and included within the contradicted text. This represents a direct conflict and creates uncertainty regarding the protection of proprietary information, therefore leading to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement shall be one (1) year, or as extended by written modification.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement shall be at BATTELLE's discretion, with no requirement for written modification.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying the agreement's term to 'BATTELLE's discretion' directly contradicts the initial one-year term and the requirement for written extensions. This in-text contradiction impacts how long the overall NDA applies, especially in relation to article 3 and confidentiality period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Washington and any action brought to enforce any provision or obligation hereunder shall be brought in a court of competent jurisdiction in the State of Washington. The prevailing party in any such proceeding shall be entitled to receive from the other party all reasonable attorneys' fees incurred by such prevailing party and all costs reasonably incurred in connection therewith. The term of this Agreement shall be at BATTELLE's discretion, with no requirement for written modification. Article 3 shall survive termination. The effective date of this Agreement shall be determined by the date affixed hereto by the party last signing this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a one-year term or extension by written modification. The changed text alters this to BATTELLE's discretion without requiring written modification. The contradicted text includes the new, discretionary term. This creates a direct conflict because the original agreement had a defined term while the new version grants BATTELLE unilateral control over the term's duration, directly contradicting the earlier fixed term and creating an ambiguity regarding the length of the agreement, thus fitting the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: During this period, BATTELLE shall not divulge such information to any third party or use such information for any purpose other than review and evaluation without the prior written consent of ADC.
perturbation.changed_text: During this period, BATTELLE may divulge such information to any third party and use such information for any purpose without the consent of ADC.
perturbation.explanation: Reversing the restriction on divulging information to third parties directly opposes the core purpose of a non-disclosure agreement. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty about the obligations placed on BATTELLE and goes directly against the first item stated within Article 1 about the purpose of the agreement
perturbation.contradicted_text: "Proprietary Information" is confidential and proprietary information relating to: 1) Protection of employee medical records relative to drug testing results, 2) Protection of medical records relative to infectious disease testing, 3) Protection of genetic testing records, and 4) Biometric identification of patients and results in the fields of pharmacy, blood banking, radiology and laboratory specimens; and related projects.
perturbation.justification: The original text prevented BATTELLE from divulging information to third parties, which aligns with the confidential nature of 'Proprietary Information' as defined in the contradicted text. The changed text explicitly allows BATTELLE to divulge information to any third party without ADC's consent. This directly contradicts the intent of protecting 'Proprietary Information' outlined in the contradicted text, creating a clear and direct conflict by allowing the disclosure of what should be kept confidential.
